Eye of Cazic-Thule  
 

Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FACE
AC: 15
STA: +5 CHA: -25 WIS: +5 INT: +5 HP: +50 MANA: +100
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Mana Regeneration: +1
WT: 0.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Eye of Cazic-Thule
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: FACE
AC: 15
STA: +5 CHA: -25 WIS: +5 INT: +5 HP: +50 MANA: +100
SV FIRE: +5 SV DISEASE: +5 SV COLD: +5 SV MAGIC: +5 SV POISON: +5
Mana Regeneration: +1
WT: 0.5 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Light:Paw of Opalla
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Vamphiri
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-17 00:02:11
Page Updated:Tue May 2nd, 2017

*This item is the result of a raid event.
Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 55
[Drops | Comments ]

This item is found on creatures.

Plane of Fear
NPC Name
Cazic Thule

Plane of Fear 2.0
NPC Name

Plane of Fear 2.0

« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
Classes
QuoteReply
# Jan 17 2004 at 12:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1595780131429
464 posts
*blink* All classes are needed just much as the next one, granted some are more needed then others any day of the week (cleric, chanty, shammys are always in short supply on 7th hammer) But to say a ranger is always much more needed then an SK/Pally is just retarded, it depends on the situation, like say I know alot of higher end guilds who use SK's for tanks over warriors on SOME MOBS (i said SOME not ALL) because we can use Fearless disc, I also know alot of higher end mobs that use SK's over rangers as pullers because we can walk right up, chain cast aggro spells out the wazoo on a mob, and get CoH'd back, and not have to deal with gaurds, just let the raidmob warp back. All classes have theyre purpose and to say one is more needed then another is a retarded statement, some are more overpowered then other classes i'll agree on that, in the end game every class is needed. Thats why the classes were designed they way the were, Now I dont see why SoE is bringing us the "Berserker class" instead of working on classes that need it.
▲ Page top
RE: Classes
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2005 at 1:59 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592260262682
285 posts
actually some classes are not needed. ok, tell me who needs berzerkers. also wizzards/mages arent nessarily needed, i know im gonna get flamed for saying this, but its true. Truly no classes are needed because sheer numbers can make up for anything. Also almost every class can be replaced by another class. Bards/chanters mez, chanters/shamans slow/haste, shamans/cleric/druid heal druids snare/port so do wizzards. WIzzards do max dps so do mages/rouges. rangers can pull, so can bards and monks. Warriors have oppurtunity to get the max hp/ac/mitigation, but sk's/paladins come close. Warriors cant hold aggro when anyother class in the game wants to take it. Its just the facts of the game that no matter what happens, unless it is one of those 1 in a thousand encounters like damage can only be done by wizzards, you will always be able to replace other classes.

also wizzards/mages arent nessarily needed, i know im gonna get flamed for saying this, but its true.

Stacking
QuoteReply
# Jun 25 2003 at 8:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751575557
10 posts
Ok, Could anyone post information on how FT stacks with other FT Items please. I have been told 2 different ways and am now a bit confused as to how it works. Any info on this would be great, thanks.

Beltran 56 Pally TP
Rinvaran Rokstar 52 Bard TP
▲ Page top
RE: Stacking
QuoteReply
# Jun 28 2003 at 6:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250606858
52 posts
every FT item stacks with every other FT item (doesn't matter if its 2 FT1 items or 1 FT3 and 1 FT2 item) till a cap of FT15.

So collect alle FT1, FT2, FT3, FT4... items till you have a summery of FT15. Every FT point over 15 will have no effect.

cu,

# Apr 24 2002 at 7:38 PM Rating: Decent
kthomas
50 posts
ok, normally i dont go flaming other peeps, and not trying to now, but lose the monk mentality, go play any caster, when you reach about lvl 50, come back here and look it this item, this item is definately a nive item for a monk, but would be better suited to any other class cept war or rog, reason being FT1, altho, even with that clikcy effect, it would still be an awesome item for a mellee, just my opinion, but let the casters/hybrids get the equipe that suits them, then is anything is left over, distribute at random, cause im sure as a lvl 50 necro i can use the flowing thought helluva lot better then some monk :)

Remeber, its just a game. When its too much, hit ctrl+alt+del and press the button labed "Power"
Then find yourself in a new world, awesome graphics, interesting interactions, and unlimited amounts of random encounters.

Proc
QuoteReply
# Nov 13 2001 at 2:14 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762591771
12 posts
Ok, all yall that say the AC, sta, and +50 hp make this item great for melee, did you forget about the proc? Why give an item with a proc as great as this to a melee who would have no use for the proc? Give this item to anybody with mana, they are the people this item was really intended for.
▲ Page top
Why this is a necro item.
QuoteReply
# Oct 28 2001 at 10:51 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965792734
105 posts
"There is not a single class in the game that will use ALL of those stats."

True. But you know what? A necro would come mighty close. Why? Cause wis is the only thing he won't use (and neg cha, but that doesn't count since noone at all can use it).

Ac is good cause we solo alot, and thus get hit quite a bit too. But that was the solo part. Also..:

* 5sta = hp = mana = feed.
* 5int = mana = feed.
* 50hp = mana = feed.
* 100mana = feed.
* Flowing Thought I = mana = feed.
* 5 sv all = resisted spells = don't have to lifetap mob = mana = feed.

And last but not least:
Feed = heal, mez, nuke, rez, coth, rune, etc etc = that little extra which saved the day.

Necro item! :P
▲ Page top

6 posts
thank you, i would love to have this for me cleric, i mean, what caster gets beat on the most, the cleric, and he would just love to have an extra 50hp and the 100m and FT1 is absolutely excellant, next in my list would be chanters, the mezz all day and slow but have few HP, so they would get next, then wizzies, go in order of casters that get beat on the most in raids, especially if 50hp could actually save your life, my cleric has come within 25hp of dying before in a vindi raid and was only saved from another cleric on raid, in which, vindi went to the 3rd assist and raid was saved, and it was because of the 50hp on this item that i lived :)(oh yeah, 5sta helped too hehe) personally, i think this is a high agro caster item with FT and lots of mana and +5 wis/int, excellant items for all classes tho :)

Haladur Einhammer, 61 Cleric of the Chosen
Cameriel Coldfire, 51 Warrior of the Ages
Saryrn

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 04 2001 at 4:10 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) a ranger item??? Whover said there guild gave it to the Ranger is a liar. Second Rangers arent better than SK's or Pallys ive seen rogues that can out tank rangers. Yer all greedy
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 10:17 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591754627141
6 posts
Fer an item like this everyone is going to find a reason why it is good for the class they play and they should it benefits all but to say anyone doesn't need it, especially someone that loves a low charisma like a necro or a caster that loves the mana regen or a tank that needs ac, just shows how selfish most of you are. This should be for my class we need it most! Puh-lease. This is an uber item fer casters and a good item fer everyone else so stop trying to say that anyone class should get over another, especially a ranger thay ain't even that good at tanking. I'd rather have a fatty warriors soakin up damage than a petite little ranger.
▲ Page top
Who Rolls on it?
QuoteReply
# May 25 2001 at 8:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591751028954
4 posts
I don't know if I'm the only one who thought of this, but why is it that this discussion comes up over so many different items? Why can't Verant Interactive make items that solely benefit either melees or casters? If they took the +STA and +HPS off this item no warrior in the world would want this, so it would be easier to find what groups can roll on it. But you have to figure, they made this item all/all so they probably intended for warriors to be able to roll on it as well. Maybe they should have made it DRU/CLR/SHM/NEC/WIZ/MAG/ENC, and that would be even easier to follow.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 22 2001 at 2:45 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I dunno, but if the Fear spell is really based off of having a lower CHA, then maybe a Necro would get the most out of this item? Just my 2cp.
RE: Necro?
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2001 at 10:27 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591761913405
67 posts
Low CHA doesn't help you land fear better. That's just one of those retarded superstitions like Velium weapons melting if you take them into the desert...LOL
▲ Page top
RE: Necro?
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2001 at 10:38 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591760131119
View User Forum
330 posts
Yes, lower Cha means fear lands easier. It means fear lands easier on you. I have seen nothing to tell me that lower Cha helps your fear spell land. But there is every indication that higher Cha helps you resist fear (I know when I had 25 Cha, fear stuck on me a lot more. Or at least it seemed that way. May have been coincidence.)
▲ Page top
RE: Necro?
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2003 at 12:57 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Wrong. Charisma has absolutely NOTHING to do with fear. Have no idea where this rumor started, but people still try to convince me of it. Charisma has absolutely nothing to do with how often you can land fear. And how often fear can land on you dpends only on your magic resistance, and a little on your wisdom (high wisdom helps you to resist mind controlling effects, like fear and charm). Either way charisma has absolutely nothing to do with it. End the rumor now, hehe, this rumor has been disproven a long time ago, but is still being spread.

It was just coincidence that fear SEEMED (that's the key word, "seemed," I guarantee if you actually quantified this, it would turn out not to be true) to land on you more often with lower charsima.

Edited, Sat Jan 25 00:30:36 2003
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 20 2001 at 1:17 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok, this item can be used by all, no doubt. But who will benefit most from it? Hybrids, Int casters and priests. It's hard to say who benefits THE MOST, Int Casters/Priests or Hybrids, but I think that pure melee should be absolute last to role for sure. The 100 mana and breeze add on is very very useful. As a melee I abhor when casters/priests role for haste items. In fact it makes me SICK. In the same token I think melee should never roll for flowing thought items.
#REDACTED, Posted: May 18 2001 at 10:03 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok, if any class can use any class should roll.
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 22 2001 at 10:50 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) If there was an earring with 7 int, 50 strength, 200 health and 20 ac, I wouldn't say "OMG 7 int r00ls for my ear!" I would let the melees have it. That would be nothing, compared to this. All the other stats WAY WAY outweigh the 15 ac. Even more than 50 str 200 health and 20ac outweighs 7 int. I wouldn't **** myself over the 15 ac if I was a monk who wasn't greedy (there are a few, I know) I would be asking "Why the hell was this awarded to me?". Heck, I wouldn't complain, even, if a bard got this. Assuming that flowing thought works to regen bard mana. (I know my mana dumping spells don't work on bards for some reason)
« Previous 1 2 Next »
